expectations.text: Acknowledges getting weaker during the cut
expectations.evidence: Provides general advice about strength loss during cuts but doesn't relay personal experience of getting weaker.

expectations.text: Attributes much of the strength loss to disrupted training (injury) rather than fat loss alone
expectations.evidence: Does not mention any specific injury disrupting training. Discusses general principles.

expectations.text: Mentions a specific injury (wrist/elbow) that limited training
Notes that strength rebounded quickly once consistent lifting resumed
expectations.evidence: Discusses general rebound but not from personal injury experience.

expectations.text: Frames consistency and acceptance as more important than chasing perfect in-cut numbers
expectations.evidence: States psychological component matters and maintaining mindset. 'Lifters who expect to lose strength often unconsciously reduce effort.'
